title: cotula pruinosa
intro: Cotula pruinosa, also known as the dusty miller cotula, is a small, annual or biennial plant native to Australia and parts of Asia, characterized by its greyish-green leaves and small yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Cotula pruinosa include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and ease respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Cotula pruinosa can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
